title: Symmetry in Nature
excerpt: An exploration of how symmetry principles manifest in natural phenomena, from crystal structures to biological forms and even in our everyday games.
content:
# Symmetry in Nature

Symmetry is one of those ideas that appears both visually and algebraically. A square, for example, can be rotated by $90^\circ$, $180^\circ$, $270^\circ$, or $360^\circ$ and still look unchanged.

The set of these transformations forms a group. If $G$ is a group acting on an object $X$, then symmetry asks which transformations preserve the structure of $X$.

$$
G = \{g : X \to X \mid g \text{ preserves structure}\}
$$

That little definition is a bridge between geometry, algebra, physics, and even political systems where invariance and structure matter.
